name: Pico Peñarroya Loop
name_original: Alto del Peñarroya — circular por la Sierra de Gúdar
description: This difficult 24.1-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Gúdar climbs to Alto del Peñarroya, offering panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Gúdar offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Gúdar mountain range, part of the Iberian System. The region features mountainous terrain, forests, natural springs, and streams, providing varied environments for cycling. Its highest peak, Peñarroya, reaches over 2,000 meters, contributing to significant elevation changes on many routes. This area is well-suited for mountain biking due to its natural features and established trails. Best mountain bike trails around Gúdar The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico Peñarroya Loop, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain towards the region's highest peak. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Alto Guadalope Enduro Trail, a moderate 35.8 miles (57.6 km) path. This route explores the terrain around the Alto Guadalope, known for its enduro-style sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Virgen de la Vega – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Urbanización Sol y Nieve, a 18.7 miles (30.2 km) trail leading through mountain scenery and offering summit viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Gúdar is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and high peaks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Gúdar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 90 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gúdar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Gúdar?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Gúdar, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels. The region features diverse landscapes, from dense forests to high peaks, providing varied environments for cycling.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Gúdar?
A: Yes, Gúdar offers a few easier routes, with 2 trails specifically rated as easy. Many of the 31 moderate trails can also be suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride, depending on their experience. These routes often explore the region's natural springs and streams, providing scenic and enjoyable rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Gúdar?
A: The terrain in Gúdar is primarily mountainous, dominated by the Sierra de Gúdar range. You can expect a mix of dense forests, natural springs, and streams, with significant elevation changes, especially on routes leading to peaks like Peñarroya. The region's diverse landscapes offer everything from technical sections to more flowing paths.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Gúdar?
A: Yes, Gúdar offers several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003664299/pico-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 24.1-mile (38.8 km) route that takes you around the region's highest peak. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Vega – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Urbanización Sol y Nieve&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 18.7-mile (30.2 km) trail offering stunning summit viewpoints.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Gúdar?
A: While mountain biking in Gúdar, you can encounter several natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5805644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Summit and Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views. The Hoya del Colmillo region is known for its striking scenery, including features like the Fuen Roya and Las Lavaderas Waterfall. You might also pass through areas with natural springs and lush greenery maintained by frequent streams.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Gúdar?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Gúdar is generally outside of the winter months. The region experiences a temperate-cold climate with frequent snow and rain, especially in winter. Spring, summer, and autumn offer more favorable conditions, though higher elevations can still be cool. During the off-season, the Valdelinares ski resort parking lots are often open for adventurers to embark on bike rides.

Q: Can I find e-bike rentals or guided tours in the Gúdar region?
A: Yes, the Gúdar region is well-equipped for cycling enthusiasts. Visitors can find options for e-bike rentals and guided tours to explore the diverse landscapes and natural beauty of the area. This makes it accessible for a wider range of riders to enjoy the trails.

Q: Are there any historical sites or towns worth visiting near the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. The beautiful town of Alcalá de la Selva is a notable historical site, featuring an ancient castle of Muslim and medieval origin, along with the Hermitages of Nuestra Señora de Loreto and San Roque, and the Church of San Simón and San Judas. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva Castle&lt;/a&gt; as a highlight. Mora de Rubielos also boasts a significant castle.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Gúdar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Gúdar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the dense forests, and the challenge of routes leading to high peaks like Peñarroya. The variety of options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels also contributes to its appeal.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Gúdar offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003664299/pico-penarroya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Peñarroya Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 24.1-mile (38.8 km) trail with significant elevation gain towards the region's highest peak. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38143980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gudar Pass – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Gúdar&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 19.9-mile (32.0 km) route that also offers substantial climbing.

Q: Is Gúdar a good destination for astro-tourism after a day of biking?
A: Yes, Gúdar is recognized as a Starlight destination due to its high altitude and clear skies, making it ideal for astro-tourism. After a day of mountain biking, you can enjoy stargazing and exploring the night sky, adding another unique dimension to your visit.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Gúdar?
A: Parking is generally available at trailheads and in nearby villages. For routes around Valdelinares, the ski resort's parking lots are often open during the off-season, providing convenient access to trails. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most suitable parking locations.

faqs.question: What is the estimated riding time for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated riding time for the Alto del Peñarroya circular route is approximately 3 hours and 27 minutes. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult. It covers a distance of nearly 39 km with over 940 meters of elevation gain, and the terrain can be technically demanding with rocky sections. It requires good to very good fitness and advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Alto del Peñarroya mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, mountain biking trails in the Sierra de Gúdar typically have designated parking areas near trailheads in towns or villages. It's advisable to check local information or the Komoot tour map for the most convenient starting point and parking options.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Alto del Peñarroya mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied terrain, starting with doubletrack that may narrow into forest singletrack. Some sections are smooth and flowy, while others can be quite varied, featuring rocky parts, steps, and even ravines (barrancas). Routes to Pico Peñarroya often follow well-marked tracks and roads, but some parts of the Sierra de Gúdar can be technically demanding.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points. The Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint offers exceptional panoramic vistas as Pico Peñarroya is the highest point in Teruel. You may also pass by the La Chaparrilla Refuge and ride through the scenic Cerro de la Maza Pine Forest. The Sierra de Gúdar is known for its picturesque landscapes, including extensive pine forests, river sources, and 'postcard' meadows.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Alto del Peñarroya circular route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gúdar region experiences varying conditions. While specific seasonal recommendations for this route are not detailed, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring snow, especially at higher elevations like Pico Peñarroya, making some sections impassable.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails in the Sierra de Gúdar?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several other trails. Notably, it covers 28% of the Sierra de Gúdar trail and 16% of the MontañasVacías route. Smaller portions also overlap with the Senda del Maestrazgo (8%), the [CIMA TE01] Peñarrota - Estación de Valdelinares * Virgen de la Vega (5%), and the Pista a Pico Peñarroya (8%).

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Gúdar?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Sierra de Gúdar. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of private property. There are no indications of fees for this particular route.

faqs.question: Is the Alto del Peñarroya mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails in the Sierra de Gúdar can vary. It's recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to always clean up after your pet. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should pack essential mountain biking gear including a helmet, gloves, appropriate clothing, and repair kit. Additionally, carry plenty of water, energy snacks, a first-aid kit, navigation tools (like a GPS device or the Komoot app), and sun protection. Due to varied terrain, consider knee and elbow pads.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the Alto del Peñarroya route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gúdar is known for its crystalline springs and river sources. However, relying solely on natural water sources can be risky. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire 39 km ride, especially since the route is difficult and can be demanding. Refuges like La Chaparrilla might offer water, but this should not be assumed.
